response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and staff software engineer with a strong web, mobile, and tooling background, but his profile has virtually no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware-software co-design, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture, and deep-learning workload performance analysis — none of which appear in his resume. His closest adjacent experience is low-latency application development in DLang at Sociomantic and some C/C++ work at National Instruments, but neither involves silicon modeling, hardware performance analysis, or AI workload optimization at the level required for a principal IC role.
strengths: Long career (30+ years) demonstrating sustained technical growth and adaptability across languages and domains
Low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, high-performance databases) shows some performance-oriented thinking
Recent involvement in AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks indicates familiarity with AI tooling ecosystems
concerns: Zero SoC, IP modeling, or hardware architecture experience — the core technical requirement of the role
No background in deep neural network training/inference optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD workloads
Skill set is almost entirely web/application-layer (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB), with no hardware-software co-design exposure

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or GPU architecture background
